response:
# PR Review: 22 findings, 0 high · 13 medium · 9 low

No high-severity findings, so nothing is a hard stop. I'm using **block merge** only for findings that represent a concrete bug or false-safety pattern, not risk *signals*.

## 🛑 Block merge (3)

| ID | Charge | Sev / agent_risk | Why block |
|---|---|---|---|
| 00008 | False Identity — `calculateRefundPreview` does `saveRefundAudit` + `sendRefundEmail` | med / 0.52 | The name promises a pure read; the body mutates and sends mail. This is the exact pattern agents misuse most — call it from a render path or a retry, and you get duplicate refunds/emails. Rename or extract before merge. |
| 00011 | Logic in the Alibi — *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves"* (billing.ts:39) | med / 0.50 | An authorization rule that lives only in prose, on a file already flagged for side-effect-in-preview (00008). If the code doesn't encode it, real money moves under the wrong conditions. Encode as a guard + test, don't ship the comment as policy. |
| 00006 | Test That Proves Nothing — `renders the enterprise plan` has 0 assertions | med / 0.46 | A green test with no `expect` is worse than no test: it shows up as coverage and protects nothing. Same file (`billing.test.ts`) is the only test file for the riskiest module. Either assert observable behaviour or delete it; don't merge a placebo. |

## ⚠️ Warn (13)

Genuine risk, worth filing fixes for — but no immediate bug.

| ID | Charge | Sev / agent_risk | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 00002 | Missing Agent Context | med / 0.61 | High agent_risk; every agent edit starts blind. Add AGENTS.md before the next agent-assisted PR. |
| 00004 | Temporal Recklessness (billing.ts × 7) | med / 0.53 | Billing + wall clock + no clock injection → flaky/untestable. |
| 00005 | Temporal Recklessness (date.ts × 5) | med / 0.61 | Same charge, dedicated date module that doesn't abstract `now()` — that's the whole point of the module. |
| 00007 | String Sprinkles — `"enterprise"` in 4 files | med / 0.60 | Plan literal scattered across api/billing/jobs/ui. Next "enterprise" rule will diverge. |
| 00009 | Concept Alias Drift — tenant/account/org/team/workspace | med / 0.58 | 5 files, 4 vocabularies for one concept. Agents will accidentally fork logic. |
| 00010 | God File — billing.ts at 310 LOC | med / 0.50 | Just over threshold; flag, don't block. Split when 00004/00008/00011 fixes happen. |
| 00012 | Duplicated Navigation Source — `/settings/billing` labelled both "Plans" and "Billing" | med / 0.57 | User-visible inconsistency; close call vs. block. Warn because no functional break — but fix before docs ship. |
| 00013 | TODO Density — todo.ts at 424/1k LOC | med / 0.57 | Filename is suspicious — likely a scratch file. Triage or delete. |
| 00014 | Route Metadata Drift — `/settings/billing` has 4 competing labels | med / 0.56 | Same root cause as 00012; bundle fixes. |
| 00003 | Commented-Out Corpse (billing.ts:33–37) | med / 0.53 | Dead `legacyRefund` snippet in the very file that has the refund bug magnet. Delete to reduce noise around 00008. |
| 00018 | Return Shape Roulette — `resolvePaymentResult` | low / 0.48 | Low severity but 0% key overlap between branches → callers will guess wrong. Worth a type before someone extends it. |
| 00021/22/23 | Orphaned Destinations — `/account/subscription`, `/team`, `/workspace/members` | low / 0.46 each | Three unreachable routes; could be in-flight (related to 00009 alias drift) or dead. Confirm intent — don't silently ship. |

## ✅ Ignore (5)

Low severity, low agent_risk, no nearby coupling to a blocked finding.

| ID | Charge | Sev / agent_risk | Why ignore |
|---|---|---|---|
| 00015 | Docs-Code Drift — broken link `./setup.md` | low / 0.51 | Single doc link; cosmetic. |
| 00016 | Weak test matcher in `creates an invoice` | low / 0.34 | Lowest agent_risk in the report; not a falsifying assertion, just a weak one. |
| 00017 | Option Bag Junk Drawer — `describeCheckout(options)` | low / 0.48 | Local to `petty.ts`; small surface. |
| 00019 | Negative Flag Maze — `!disableBilling && !skipRetry` | low / 0.47 | Two negatives, not a thicket. |
| 00020 | TODO Density — billing.ts at 12.9/1k | low / 0.40 | Barely over threshold; once 00013/00010 are addressed this disappears in noise. |

## How I drew the lines

- **Block:** finding describes a *concrete* false-safety pattern (test that asserts nothing, name that lies, rule that isn't enforced). Severity alone wasn't enough — none of the findings are high — so I treated `agent_risk` as a tiebreaker only when combined with a real bug surface.
- **Warn:** medium severity, *or* low severity + agent_risk ≥ 0.46 + part of a cluster (route/nav drift, tenant aliases). These compound — each one alone is small, the cluster isn't.
- **Ignore:** low severity, isolated, agent_risk ≤ 0.51, no coupling to a blocked item.

**One pattern worth calling out:** `billing.ts` carries 5 of the 22 findings (00003, 00004, 00008, 00010, 00011, 00020) including both block-level ones. Treat that file as the focus of the fix-up PR rather than scattering 22 small edits.
